body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: unknown; driver_capability: ready
applicable: yes

## Reasoning

`long-agentic-session-stress` (scenario-meanings.md): the defining behavior is a long agentic turn carrying a large volume of interleaved assistant fragments and tool-call/result pairs, validating that state tracking stays coherent across the burst — within the turn state stays continuously `working` (no flicker to ready/waiting between tool calls), the turn settles cleanly to `ready`, and the session is never stuck. Primitive exercised: `tool_calls` + `headless_mode`, stress-testing the parser's ability to stay coherent across many sequential function_call/function_call_output pairs in ONE turn. Explicitly *Not to be confused with* `autonomous-loop` (no user prompts between iterations) or `self-correction-iteration` (a short retry cycle within one turn, not a high-volume burst).

**agent_supports: yes.** Codex CLI 0.130.0 drives shell work through its exec/`function_call` tool and runs many sequential commands autonomously inside a single turn with NO permission gate (capabilities.json permission_hooks:false), so the agent never stops to ask the user mid-burst. A prompt that asks it to explore a directory — pwd, then ls -la, then wc -l on several files, then summarize — deterministically produces a chain of many function_call/function_call_output pairs in one turn before the final task_complete. This is exactly the high-volume single-turn burst the scenario targets, and unlike claudecode (which refuses auto-mode for paid models and stalls tool-call turns at a permission gate, forcing the claudecode recipe into a 4-plain-turn proxy), codex can exercise the real many-tool-call shape natively. capabilities.json records tool_calls:true, headless_mode:true, tui:true.

**daemon_capability: unknown; driver_capability: ready.** The codex parser (parser.go) models each tool call as a paired `function_call` (open) / `function_call_output` (close) event, and a completed command always emits its function_call_output which CLOSES the open call (HasOpenToolCall clears), so the classifier never treats an intermediate tool result as a `waiting` gate or a `ready` settle — the whole burst stays a single contiguous `working` span. The turn settles to `ready` only at the canonical `task_complete` → `turn_done` signal (parser.go lines 155-170), whose explicit purpose is to suppress the assistant_message working→ready→working flicker that intermediate assistant fragments would otherwise cause at the start of every turn. Net observable: one ready→working at the prompt, ONE contiguous working span across the entire many-call burst, one working→ready at task_complete, final ready. The `partial` (matching the coverage matrix) reflects the documented dependence on the `task_complete` turn-done signal: if codex ever omits or delays task_complete (the fallback risk noted in the matrix re: #105 stale-tool timer flicker, since closed), the daemon would fall back to assistant_message heuristics and could mis-time the final settle — so the cell is observable but rests on one load-bearing signal rather than redundant evidence.

This cell is observationally DISTINCT from the neighbouring `codex × self-correction-iteration` (a short failing-then-corrected two-cycle retry asserting continuity across ONE errored result) and `codex × auto-executed-tool-call` (a single happy-path open/close): this cell asserts the parser's coherence across a HIGH VOLUME of sequential ungated tool calls in one turn — the stress dimension neither neighbour exercises.

## Conclusion

Applicable: yes. codex natively runs a long single-turn tool-call burst with no permission gate, and the daemon observes the full arc as one contiguous working span (each function_call_output closes its open call; task_complete is the only ready settle). Recipe: an interactive prompt that induces many sequential exec calls in one turn (pwd → ls -la → wc -l on the first few files → summarize), wait_turn (generous timeout for a longer turn), trailing sleep. Spec asserts user-observable state/topology only — session-birth ready, one ready→working, a contiguous working dwell across the burst with no flicker to ready/waiting mid-turn, one working→ready, final ready — never specific tool output text, command count, or token totals (all model/OS-dependent).
caveats: Assert user-observable state/topology only — do NOT assert specific exec tool output text, the exact number of commands codex chooses to run, or token totals; all are model/OS-dependent and not stable across re-records.

irrlicht_observes is partial (matching the coverage matrix): coherence of the burst rests on the single task_complete→turn_done settle signal; the no-mid-turn-flip invariant is the load-bearing assertion and is exactly what the codex open-call + turn_done logic guarantees.

Distinct from the committed codex × self-correction-iteration (short two-cycle retry) and codex × auto-executed-tool-call (single happy-path call): this cell stresses coherence across a HIGH VOLUME of sequential tool calls in one turn.

sources.note: long-agentic-session-stress: a realistic high-volume turn validates state tracking stays coherent — within a turn state stays working (no flicker), clean working→ready at turn boundaries, never stuck; primitive = tool_calls + headless_mode; distinct from autonomous-loop and self-correction-iteration
tool_calls:true, headless_mode:true, tui:true, permission_hooks:false — codex runs many sequential ungated tool calls autonomously inside one turn

function_call/function_call_output modeled as paired open/close; a completed command's function_call_output clears HasOpenToolCall (no waiting); task_complete→turn_done (lines 155-170) is the only ready settle, suppressing intermediate assistant_message flicker so the whole burst is one contiguous working span
coverage cell codex × long-agentic-session-stress = agent_supports:yes / irrlicht_observes:partial (#105 stale-tool timer flicker closed; task_complete dependence creates fallback risk)
neighbouring tool-call cell: short failing-then-corrected two-cycle retry asserting no-flicker across ONE errored result; this cell adds the high-volume stress dimension
